cards.prompt: The political movement led by Lenin based on the idea that a disciplined revolutionary party could overthrow capitalism on behalf of the working class

cards.prompt: Lenin (Vladimir Ilyich Ulyanov)
cards.answer: Leader of the Bolshevik Revolution (1917) and founder of the Soviet Union; adapted Marxist theory to Russian conditions and created the first communist state

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The nationalist ideology combining extreme nationalism, militarism, racial hierarchy, and anti-communism, exemplified by Mussolini's Italy and Hitler's Germany

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Adolf Hitler
cards.answer: Leader of Nazi Germany (1933–1945) whose expansionist and racist policies led to World War II in Europe and the Holocaust, the systematic murder of six million Jews and others

cards.prompt: The Nazi German program of systematic murder of European Jews and other groups, killing approximately six million people

cards.prompt: The atomic bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, Japan

cards.prompt: The policy of appeasement by which Britain and France allowed Hitler to annex Czechoslovakia (1938), hoping to avoid another world war

cards.prompt: The Communist Party takes power in China, founding the People's Republic

cards.prompt: President Wilson's 1918 proposal for post-war peace based on national self-determination, open diplomacy, and a League of Nations
